requirement: Plan a follow-up light-mode and shared-UI refinement pass covering these 16 issues only: 1) light-mode stepper-control stepper-btn hover is too subtle, 2) improve toggle-group toggle-btn hover in both dark/light mode, 3) adjust --surface-card-top so it is not pure white, 4) light-mode CAGED shapes are too subtle, 5) light-mode fret wire shadow is too noticeable and fret wire color could match dark mode, 6) light-mode strings are too dark, 7) light-mode inlay dots are too subtle and should read white/pearl, 8) light-mode fretboard background should move toward maple with #F1C38E, 9) circle of fifths in light mode can lose visible borders on the active/root slice depending on which segment is selected, 10) degree-chip-strip chips have glow in light mode, 11) light-mode nut gradient is too noticeable and should be bone white, 12) theory-card disclosure button focus ring should be wider to match the hover-state radius in both modes, 13) fretboard hover state is too subtle in both modes, 14) light-mode headstock should use wood color instead of white/page background, 15) light-mode fretboard note ring colors are too hard to differentiate and ring thickness is too thin, 16) light-mode scale degree colors should use white labels like dark mode.

r1.repo_shape: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite app, with styling split across src/styles/{tokens,semantic,themes,App}.css and CSS Modules in src/components/**. The original checkout is currently clean on light-mode-warm-pass/feat-light-mode-palette.
local_instructions: Use conventional commits and conventional PR titles.
R1 must stay in the original checkout and remain read-only.
The repo lint path uses eslint plus stylelint via npm run lint.
The repo already has CSS-module selector tests, so shared CSS refactors may need to preserve selector assumptions.
likely_write_scopes: Shared theme/token scope: src/styles/tokens.css, src/styles/semantic.css, src/styles/themes.css
CAGED shape contrast scope: src/components/FretboardSVG/FretboardShapeLayer.tsx, src/shapes, and shared light-mode tokens
conflict_risks: --surface-card-top is reused by cards, strips, and overlays through semantic aliases, so changing it will move more than one surface.
shared.module.css is a high-coupling file because ToggleBar, Stepper, NoteGrid, and other controls compose from it.

Fretboard wood, strings, fret wire, nut, inlay, headstock, and note-ring styling are split between theme tokens and hardcoded SVG defs/background rendering, so one-sided changes can leave inconsistent output.
Degree-color and note-ring tokens affect multiple surfaces, including fretboard notes, degree chips, and chord-practice strip states.

Circle-of-fifths stroke visibility and fretboard note-ring contrast both depend on light-mode stroke/readability against near-white surfaces, so small token shifts can create slice-specific or note-role-specific regressions.
The existing branch name indicates prior light-mode palette work, which raises merge-overlap risk if this plan assumes a separate workstream.
worktree_signals: Parallelism is only medium because several surfaces are partially independent, but they converge on the same shared token files.
The original checkout is clean, which lowers the need for worktrees from a safety standpoint.
The requirement is a broad visual sweep across 16 issues, so sequential execution is likely safer unless the user explicitly wants isolated token vs component work.
The current checkout is already on a light-mode feature branch, which increases overlap risk between any follow-up workstream and existing light-mode changes.
unknowns: Whether this follow-up should extend the current light-mode workstream/branch or be planned as a separate follow-up workstream.
Whether white note labels should apply only to scale-degree color mode or to all light-mode fretboard note labels.
Whether note-ring differentiation and thicker strokes should apply across all light-mode note roles and lens states or only the standard non-lens note states.

assumptions: This pass is limited to the 16 enumerated issues and any required test, snapshot, or token-routing updates directly needed to support them.
No layout or structural redesign is intended; minimal CSS or SVG rule changes are allowed where required to fix visibility, stroke, or token routing issues.
Dark-mode changes are intentional only for the explicitly cross-theme items (toggle hover, theory disclosure focus ring, fretboard hover) and any unavoidable shared-token fallout they require.
Visual regression and theme-contract updates are expected because several items target shared tokens and highly visible fretboard/circle surfaces.

questions.question: Should this follow-up be planned as an extension of the current light-mode workstream, or as a separate follow-up workstream?
questions.why: R1 found the original checkout is already clean on light-mode-warm-pass/feat-light-mode-palette, and the requested files overlap heavily with the same shared token and fretboard files touched by the existing light-mode area.

options.label: Extend the current light-mode workstream
options.description: Plan this as additional work on the existing light-mode branch/PR path so overlapping theme and fretboard files stay in one sequence.

options.label: Create a separate follow-up workstream
options.description: Plan a new branch/PR after or beside the current branch, accepting extra branch and merge coordination to keep this scope isolated.

questions.question: When you say light-mode scale degree colors should use white labels like dark mode, should that apply only to degree-color mode, or to all light-mode fretboard note labels?
questions.why: R1 found degree-color mode currently combines bright note fills with dark text by default, while ordinary light-mode fretboard notes also use dark labels for readability on the maple board. Broadening this choice changes more than just scale-degree color mode.

options.label: Degree-color mode only
options.description: Use white labels only when scale-degree colors are active, while keeping standard light-mode fretboard note labels dark for general board readability.

options.label: All light-mode fretboard note labels
options.description: Switch the full light-mode fretboard to white note labels, accepting a broader readability and contrast change beyond scale-degree color mode.

questions.question: Should the note-ring differentiation pass cover all light-mode note roles and practice-lens states, or just the standard non-lens note states you see most often?
questions.why: R1 found ring colors and stroke widths are shared across tonic, active, scale-only, color-tone, degree-color, and lens-specific states. Limiting the scope could leave inconsistent thickness or contrast in guide-tone and tension views.

options.label: Cover all light-mode note roles
options.description: Make ring color separation and stroke weight consistent across standard and lens-specific light-mode note states, accepting broader selector and snapshot churn.

options.label: Limit to standard note states
options.description: Tune the common tonic, active, scale-only, and color-tone states first, leaving guide-tone and tension-specific sub-states unchanged unless they obviously regress.

acceptance_criteria: The 16 enumerated issues are addressed without redesigning layout or component structure.
Shared control hover and focus polish improves the light-mode stepper, both-theme toggle hover, both-theme theory disclosure focus ring, and both-theme fretboard hover affordance.
Light-mode top-level card surfaces no longer read as pure white where --surface-card-top currently fans out.
Light-mode fretboard materials move closer to the requested maple direction, including wood tone, strings, fret wire/shadow balance, nut, headstock, and inlay visibility.
Light-mode CAGED shapes are visibly clearer on the fretboard.
Light-mode circle-of-fifths active/root slice borders remain visually complete across slice selections.
Light-mode degree-chip-strip styling removes the current glow mismatch relative to the fretboard style.
Light-mode fretboard note rings are more distinguishable and thicker across all light-mode note roles and practice-lens states.
White note labels apply in degree-color mode only, while standard light-mode fretboard note labels remain dark unless required by another accepted issue.
Relevant theme-contract expectations and visual snapshots are updated intentionally.

execution_strategy.shape: single sequential chunk on the current light-mode workstream
execution_strategy.rationale: The aligned scope extends the existing light-mode branch and touches the same high-coupling theme, fretboard, and snapshot surfaces. Splitting this into parallel chunks would create overlapping writes in shared token files and stacked verification churn without strong isolation benefits.

chunks.name: Light-mode follow-up refinement pass
chunks.goal: Implement the 16 confirmed light-mode and shared-UI follow-up refinements on the existing light-mode workstream, including the required contract-test and visual-snapshot updates.
context: Extend the existing light-mode workstream instead of creating a separate branch/PR chain.
Apply white labels only to degree-color mode, not to all standard light-mode fretboard note labels.
Cover all light-mode note roles and practice-lens states when improving note-ring differentiation and ring thickness.
implementation: Retune shared control hover and focus styling for the stepper, toggle groups, theory disclosure button, and fretboard hover affordance where requested.
Adjust light-mode shared surface tokens such as --surface-card-top and any directly dependent chrome needed to satisfy the confirmed visibility issues.
Refine the light-mode fretboard materials and overlays, including maple wood tone, strings, fret wire/shadow balance, nut, headstock, inlays, and CAGED shape visibility.
Fix the light-mode circle-of-fifths active/root border visibility and remove light-mode degree-chip glow mismatches.
Improve light-mode fretboard note-ring differentiation and stroke weight across all light-mode note roles and lens states, while limiting white note labels to degree-color mode only.
Update the relevant theme-contract assertions and refresh the affected visual snapshots.
chunks.commit: feat: refine light mode follow-up surfaces and fretboard contrast
chunks.pr_title: feat: refine light mode follow-up surfaces and fretboard contrast
chunks.pr_body:
Summary:
- refine shared hover/focus chrome and light-mode card surfaces
- improve light-mode fretboard materials, note rings, caged shapes, and degree-color readability
- fix light-mode circle/degrees styling inconsistencies and update affected tests/snapshots

Verification:
- npm run lint
- npm run test
- npm run test:e2e -- e2e/theme-contract.spec.ts
- npm run test:visual

Risks:
- shared token changes fan out into cards, strips, and control surfaces
- fretboard rendering is split across tokens, CSS, and hardcoded SVG defs
- visual snapshot churn is expected for light-mode fretboard and circle surfaces
